name: Lesotho

colors.symbolism: rain and the sky; in the national motto, an essential blessing
the indigenous black African heritage of the Basotho nation (the central mokorotlo hat)

description: Three horizontal stripes — blue, white and green — in proportions roughly 3:4:3, with a black mokorotlo (the traditional conical straw hat of the Basotho) centred on the white middle stripe.
history: Adopted on 4 October 2006, the fortieth anniversary of Lesotho's independence from the United Kingdom. The current flag replaced a 1987 design that had carried a Basotho shield, assegai and knobkerrie (military symbols introduced after a coup); the 2006 redesign deliberately replaced the weapons with the peaceful mokorotlo to mark the country's commitment to non-violence and stability. An earlier 1966–1987 flag had also used the mokorotlo emblem.

features.symbolism: blue for rain (vital in this mountainous country), white for peace, green for prosperity; together expressing the national motto Khotso, Pula, Nala ('Peace, Rain, Prosperity')
the Basotho nation and its peaceful traditions; a culturally specific national symbol replacing the assegai-and-shield emblem of the previous flag
